title: NOD-EEG

funding_references.funder_name: National Natural Science Foundation of China
National Natural Science Foundation of China
Ministry of Science and Technology of China

description: The Natural Object Dataset-EEG (NOD-EEG) is a multimodal neuroimaging dataset integrating electroencephalography (EEG) data collected from subjects viewing naturalistic object images from ImageNet. This dataset complements previously collected fMRI and MEG data from the same subjects and stimuli, enabling investigation of neural mechanisms underlying object recognition with high temporal resolution. The dataset includes raw EEG recordings, preprocessed data, and epoched data across multiple sessions and runs, along with comprehensive trial metadata and stimulus information.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from subjects viewing naturalistic stimulus images from ImageNet. Raw data are stored in BIDS format with multiple sessions per subject. Data were preprocessed using MNE (version 1.7.1) and are available as both continuous time series and epoched formats. Trial information including stimulus category and subject responses are documented in detailed event files.
